text.title: ai-accessibility-automation-2026 Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-accessibility-automation-2026 as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running Playwright scanner without handling cookie consent banners and auth walls first
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Scanners that skip consent/auth produce incomplete results — they scan only the pre-auth or pre-consent state of the page.

failure-mode.description: Scanning SPAs without explicit navigation triggers to all routes
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: axe-core scans the current DOM snapshot only; routes not visited during the scan session are not audited.

failure-mode.description: Using synchronous wait for caption API responses
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Caption APIs return files asynchronously; agents must implement polling with timeout and retry logic.

failure-mode.description: Publishing AI-generated VPAT without human legal review
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM paraphrasing of WCAG criteria introduces compliance risk; VPAT criterion language is legally precise and must match W3C wording exactly.

failure-mode.description: Assuming AI detection automatically drives compliance
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: 94.8% of homepages still fail WCAG checks despite tooling availability; process and accountability drive compliance, not automation alone.

failure-mode.description: Replacing user testing with people with disabilities using automated scanning
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Automation cannot validate cognitive accessibility, task success with real users, or actual AT compatibility — human user testing is irreplaceable.
